rulings.Case Title: HOLLY BRATT RN VS. KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Order Sustaining Demurrer To Pltf'Sthird Amended Complaint Without Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 3, 2009, line 3, PLAINTIFF HOLLY BRATT Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Sustaining Demurrer To Plaintiff's Third Amended Complaint Without Leave To Amend. Motion for reconsideration is denied. Court lacks jurisdiction to hear the motion because the complaint has been dismissed and a final judgment entered (CCP section 581d; Safeco Ins. Co. vs. Architectural Facades Unlimited, Inc. (2005) 134 Cal.App. 4th 1477, 1482. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK HELLAND VS. RAY BRONNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Motion For Sanctions & Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 3, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT RAY BRONNER Re-Notice Of Motion For Sanctions & Request For Judicial Notice. Motion is denied. Motion does not comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7 which requires party seeking sanctions to specify offensive pleading and give that party twenty-one days to withdraw. No Cited basis for sanctions for out of Court conduct. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO BY AND THROUGH VS. WILLIAM DAWSON et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 3, 2009, line 9, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Summary adjudication granted as to second, third, fourth, fifth, sixth, seventh, eighth, tenth, eleventh, twelfth, thirteenth and fourteenth affirmative defenses. Statutes cited in second affirmative defense not applicable to this action. The lease does not contain an implied covenant as alleged in the third affirmative defense. The fourth, fifth, seventh, eighth, tenth, eleventh, twelfth and fourteenth affirmative defenses do not apply to this action. (Also, waiver disallowed by lease.) No triable issue of material fact raised as to sixth and thirteenth affirmative defenses. Motion is denied as to the first affirmative defense for retaliatory eviction. Triable issue of fact raised regarding timing of eviction and defendant's actions. Motion is denied as to ninth and fifteenth affirmative defenses, failure to shift burden of proof with specially propounded interrogatories. Motion is denied for possession in view of court's ruling on affirmative defenses. =(302/CWW)
